title: Classes
description: Master OOP structures in C++ by declaring classes, encapsulating data, and using visibility access specifiers.
learningObjectives: Declare classes containing both properties and methods
Enforce data encapsulation using public, private, and protected levels
Explain memory footprints of simple structures versus classes

sections.title: Encapsulation and Blueprinting
sections.content:
A class acts as a blueprint defining properties (data members) and actions (member functions) for an object. The key paradigm is Encapsulation: hiding internal complexity and state from external entities. Access levels control this:

1. `public`: Accessible anywhere in code.
2. `private`: Accessible only within the declaring class.
3. `protected`: Accessible within the declaring class and derived classes.

sections.title: Encapsulating Character Stats
